foodServiceApplicability: Tampa food service facilities that discharge grease into the City's treatment works fall under the Grease Management Ordinance.
interceptorType: Tampa distinguishes indoor grease traps from outdoor grease interceptors and requires approved devices sized for the facility's wastewater load.
pumpOutFrequency: Indoor grease traps must be opened, inspected, cleaned, and maintained at least weekly and whenever grease plus solids exceed 25% of trap volume. Grease interceptors must be pumped when solids exceed the outlet invert, grease plus solids exceed 25% of capacity, or the interceptor stops capturing grease.
manifestRequirement: Keep pump logs and maintenance records on site for three years and use City of Tampa approved manifest forms for registered grease haulers.
submissionMethod: Use Tampa's grease ordinance workflow and confirm the service company appears on the current registered grease haulers list.
enforcementNote: Tampa can require grease trap cleaning within 24 hours, interceptor pumping within 72 hours, and continued noncompliance can escalate to city pump-out charges or code enforcement.
